old_claim.note: cas9_programmable_nuclease was previously treated as a plausible hard prerequisite for base_editing without a field-specific edge receipt.

new_claim.note: Cytosine base editors use catalytically impaired Cas9 as the programmable DNA-targeting component fused to a deaminase.

source_shape.source_locator: Abstract and editor-design description of cytosine base editors built from catalytically impaired Cas9 fused to a cytidine deaminase.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper defines base editing as a Cas9-directed editor architecture in which an impaired Cas9 supplies programmable DNA targeting while a deaminase performs base conversion.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports a required edge because removing Cas9 changes the technology boundary from base editing as described in the paper to a different DNA-targeting architecture.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Invariant preserved: base_editing -> cas9_programmable_nuclease remains required; this receipt adds source-shaped evidence for the existing CRISPR field edge.
would_reject_if: The cited paper did not describe a Cas9-based base-editor architecture.
The edge was downgraded to contextual despite Cas9 being part of the described editor system.
The current graph pointed base editing at an SSD-like application rather than the nuclease component.
short_reason: Base editing uses impaired Cas9 as its programmable DNA-targeting component.
